thread.title: More than 100 Georgians sleep outside in solidarity with youth experiencing homelessness
thread.title_full: More than 100 Georgians sleep outside in solidarity with youth experiencing homelessness

author: Sawyer Buccy
title: More than 100 Georgians sleep outside in solidarity with youth experiencing homelessness
text:
More than 100 Georgians sleep outside in solidarity with youth experiencing homelessness
ATLANTA, Ga. (Atlanta News First) - How did you sleep last night? Warm bed? Heater going?
“People always find out about young people who are experiencing homelessness and have these stereotypes of who that is, what it looks like,” said Dr. Alie Redd, CEO of Covenant House Georgia.
Close to 150 people signed up for the “sleep out.” It was a fundraiser where people slept outside for the night, raising funds for more housing options for unhoused youth and an act of solidarity with those who are still struggling.
“I don’t want to be a part of a society where young people are unhoused, I don’t want to be a part of a community where I don’t do anything,” said Scott Brown with Georgia Pacific.
At Covenant House Georgia, the organization works to get unhoused youth back up on their feet again. The message is one of hope, despite the situation that brought the young person through their doors.
“They are youth who have experienced generational poverty, they have aged out of foster care, they are running away from gang violence, domestic violence, they have been displaced from all over the country as a result of trafficking,” said Redd.
